data.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

When /dso:onboarding runs check-local-env (or the equivalent project-detection step), it should only list pyproject.toml in its 'what I found' output when the project is actually a Python project. A non-Python project (e.g. a pure Node.js or bash-only repo) should not mention pyproject.toml in the scan results.

#### Actual Behavior

/dso:onboarding lists pyproject.toml under 'what I found' even when the project has no Python code and pyproject.toml is absent or irrelevant. The presence check appears to run unconditionally rather than only when Python indicators (*.py files, requirements.txt, etc.) are detected first.
data.title: [onboarding: check-local-env]: Python project detection triggers -> pyproject.toml listed in 'what I found' for non-Python projects
